etymology.description: phonetic 爻 + semantic 子 (child) + semantic 攵 (teaching cane) -> teaching a child
etymology.interpretation: The character 教(教) (jiào, "teach") combines the phonetic element 爻(爻) with semantic elements 子(子) (zǐ, "child") and 攵(攵) (pū, "teaching cane"). The 子(子, "child") represents the learners or the ones being taught. The 攵(攵, "teaching cane") symbolizes authority and the traditional method of instruction, emphasizing discipline in teaching. Together, these elements depict the act of instructing or guiding a child, which leads to the meanings of 'teach' and by extension, 'religion' as a system of teaching beliefs.

examples.chinese: 他是一名经验丰富的教师(他是一名經驗豐富的教師)。
examples.pinyin: Tā shì yī míng jīngyàn fēngfù de jiàoshī.

examples.chinese: 佛教是世界上最古老的宗教之一(佛教是世界上最古老的宗教之一)。
examples.pinyin: Fójiào shì shìjiè shàng zuì gǔlǎo de zōngjiào zhī yī.
examples.english: Buddhism is one of the oldest religions in the world.

examples.chinese: 我的母亲教我游泳(我的母親教我游泳)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ de mǔqīn jiào wǒ yóuyǒng.
examples.english: My mother taught me how to swim.
